models.label: NequIP
models.author: Harvard (Kozinsky lab)
models.desc: E(3)-equivariant message-passing potential that set the template for data-efficient, high-accuracy force fields across molecules and materials.

models.label: Allegro
models.author: Harvard (Kozinsky lab)
models.desc: Strictly local equivariant architecture designed for massive parallel MD (100M+ atoms) while retaining NequIP-level accuracy.

models.label: Equiformer V2
models.author: Meta FAIR / MIT
models.desc: Improved equivariant transformer with higher-degree tensor representations; achieves state-of-the-art OC20/OC22 performance with strong data-efficiency.

models.label: MACE
models.author: Cambridge / Csányi group
models.desc: Higher-order equivariant message passing (4-body messages) that reaches or surpasses SOTA accuracy with only 1–2 layers and powers universal MACE-MP models.

models.label: GRACE
models.author: ICAMS
models.desc: Graph Atomic Cluster Expansion: a foundation-scale implementation of ACE with explicit multi-body basis functions for wide-coverage materials modelling.

models.label: Orb-v3
models.author: Orbital Materials
models.desc: Wide & shallow graph neural simulator with smoothed attention, heavily optimized for torch.compile and extreme throughput on large periodic systems.

models.label: OrbMol
models.author: Orbital Materials
models.desc: Orb-v3 variant for molecules, electrolytes, metal complexes, and biomolecules, trained on the ~100M-structure OMol25 dataset with explicit total-charge and spin-multiplicity conditioning.

models.label: Tensor Field Networks
models.author: Thomas et al.
models.desc: First E(3)-equivariant convolutional architecture for point clouds, introducing spherical-harmonic tensor products that underlie nearly all modern equivariant MLIPs (NequIP, MACE, Equiformer).

models.label: SE(3)-Transformer
models.author: Fuchs et al.
models.desc: Self-attention generalized to SE(3)-equivariant inputs via tensor field attention; an early blueprint for equivariant transformer architectures like Equiformer.

models.label: JMP
models.author: Shoghi et al. (CMU / Meta)
models.desc: Joint Multi-task Pretraining: trains one backbone simultaneously on OC20, OC22, ANI-1x and Transition-1x, demonstrating that multi-dataset pretraining yields strong transferable potentials — a precursor to UMA-style universal models.

models.label: eSEN
models.author: Meta FAIR
models.desc: Equivariant Smooth Energy Network: conservative-force equivariant GNN with a smooth potential energy surface designed for stable long-horizon MD. Serves as the backbone underneath Meta's UMA foundation model.

models.label: UMA
models.author: Meta FAIR
models.desc: Universal Model for Atoms: a Mixture of Linear Experts (MoLE) foundation model built on the eSEN backbone, trained on ~500M structures spanning OC20, ODAC23, OMat24, OMC25, and OMol25. NeurIPS 2025 spotlight.

models.label: Nequix
models.author: Koker & Smidt (MIT Atomic Architects)
models.desc: Compact E(3)-equivariant foundation potential that pairs a simplified NequIP design with equivariant RMS layer normalization and the Muon optimizer, reaching near-SOTA accuracy on a <125 GPU-hour training budget.

models.label: PET-MAD
models.author: EPFL (Ceriotti lab)
models.desc: Lightweight universal transformer-GNN potential (Point-Edge Transformer) trained on the Massive Atomistic Diversity (MAD) dataset of ~96k structures across 85 elements; competitive with larger uMLIPs for solids, surfaces, and molecules.

models.label: BPNN
models.author: Behler & Parrinello
models.desc: The Behler–Parrinello high-dimensional neural network potential — the 2007 paper that introduced symmetry functions and the atomic-decomposition framework underlying essentially every modern MLIP.

models.label: ACE
models.author: Drautz (ICAMS)
models.desc: Atomic Cluster Expansion: a complete, systematically improvable many-body basis for the local atomic environment; the mathematical backbone of PACE/GRACE and a strong influence on MACE.

models.label: GAP / SNAP
models.author: Cambridge / Sandia
models.desc: Gaussian Approximation Potentials and SNAP: kernel and descriptor-based MLIPs with rigorous many-body expansions, accurate but relatively expensive.

models.label: DeepMD
models.author: DeepModeling
models.desc: Deep Potential Molecular Dynamics: local frame descriptors + deep networks giving ab-initio-level accuracy with linear scaling, widely used for large MD.

models.label: DPA-2
models.author: DeepModeling
models.desc: Second-generation Deep Potential architecture with attention and multi-task heads, targeting a universal deep potential for diverse chemistries.

models.label: MatterSim
models.author: Microsoft
models.desc: Large-scale foundation model trained on millions of ab-initio trajectories, designed as a reusable simulator for materials discovery workflows.

models.label: NEP89
models.author: Chalmers (Fan group) / Liang et al.
models.desc: Universal neuroevolution-potential foundation model spanning 89 elements across inorganic and organic materials, trained via separable natural evolution strategies and distributed in GPUMD for empirical-potential-like speed.

models.label: LiTEN-FF
models.author: Zhejiang Univ. (Hou lab) / Su et al.
models.desc: Equivariant network with Linearly Tensorized Quadrangle Attention (TQA) that captures 3- and 4-body interactions in linear time; pre-trained on nablaDFT and fine-tuned on SPICE as a quantum-accurate biomolecular force-field foundation model.

models.label: ANI-2x
models.author: Isayev / Roitberg
models.desc: Accurate NeurAl networK engINe potential for organic molecules (C,H,N,O,S,F,Cl); widely used in drug discovery for fast geometry and energy scans.

models.label: SchNet
models.author: Schütt et al.
models.desc: Continuous-filter convolutional network that introduced smooth, translation-invariant filters for molecules and crystals; the baseline for many later GNN MLIPs.

models.label: DimeNet++
models.author: Gasteiger et al.
models.desc: Directional message passing network with spherical basis functions that explicitly encode bond angles, improving data efficiency over SchNet-style models.

models.label: GemNet-OC
models.author: TUM / OC20
models.desc: High-capacity spherical message-passing architecture used in the OC20/OC22 benchmarks; very strong for catalyst adsorption and surface chemistry.

models.label: PaiNN
models.author: Schütt et al.
models.desc: Polarizable Atom Interaction Neural Network: uses coupled scalar/vector features to capture forces and dipoles with E(3) equivariance at lower cost than full tensors.

models.label: SevenNet
models.author: Seoul Nat. Univ.
models.desc: Speed-optimized invariant network inspired by NequIP-style features, designed for very large simulations where throughput is more critical than strict equivariance.

models.label: ALIGNN
models.author: NIST
models.desc: Atomistic Line Graph Neural Network: augments the atomic graph with a line graph so bond angles and higher-order interactions are encoded explicitly.

models.label: M3GNet
models.author: Materials Virtual Lab
models.desc: Materials Graph Network with 3-body interactions; trained on Materials Project relaxations to give a universal potential over most of the periodic table.

models.label: CHGNet
models.author: Berkeley (Ceder group)
models.desc: Charge-aware graph neural network that extends M3GNet with oxidation state and local charge features; particularly strong for battery and redox-active materials.

models.label: SevenNet-Nano
models.author: Seoul Nat. Univ. (MDIL-SNU)
models.desc: Lightweight universal MLIP distilled from the SevenNet-Omni teacher, delivering over an order-of-magnitude speedup while retaining broad transferability for scalable atomistic simulations on thousands of atoms.

models.label: SevenNet-Omni
models.author: Seoul Nat. Univ. (MDIL-SNU)
models.desc: Multi-fidelity universal foundation MLIP built on the SevenNet-MF backbone and trained on 15 open datasets (~250M structures across molecules, crystals, and surfaces); serves as the teacher model for SevenNet-Nano.

models.label: PFP v8
models.author: Preferred Networks / Matlantis
models.desc: Eighth release of the Preferred Potential: the first universal MLIP trained on a large r2SCAN meta-GGA dataset (70 elements) atop a 96-element PBE backbone, halving melting-point error vs. PBE-trained models. Distributed commercially via the Matlantis SaaS platform.
